children.name: Introduction to the Profession
Purpose: Visualize and understand the seabed
Importance of surveying: Avoiding costly errors
The team: Surveyors, Party Chief, ROV pilots, Data Processors
Career paths: From trainee to specialist or leader
Life on the Vessel
Terminology: Bow, stern, starboard, port, galley
Communication: Clear protocols and radio etiquette
Teamwork: Trust and cross-disciplinary collaboration
Routines: Shift work (12/12) and health
Online vs Offline: Real-time acquisition vs post-processing
Safety and Environment
PPE: Hard hat, safety boots, high-visibility clothing
Emergencies: Muster stations and alarms
Environmental responsibility: Waste management and marine life
Regulations: IMCA, IHO, MARPOL
Positioning Technology
Surface: GNSS, RTK, and INS (backup and bridging)
Underwater: USBL (ultra-short baseline) and LBL (long baseline)
Motion: DVL (velocity) and MRU (pitch, roll, heave)
Sound velocity: SVP measurements for acoustic correction
Sensors and Sonars
Echo sounders: Single Beam (SBES) and Multibeam (MBES)
Seabed imaging: Side Scan Sonar (SSS)
Sub-Bottom Profiler: Sub-surface geology
Magnetometers: Detecting ferrous objects and UXO
Subsea platforms: ROV (remotely operated) and AUV (autonomous)
Workflows and Processes
Planning: Scope, equipment, and weather windows
Calibration: Patch test (MBES) and box-in (USBL)
Verification: Confirming data integrity
Data processing: Noise cleaning and 3D modeling
Reporting: Documentation of biases and results
Modern Technology and Development
AI tools: Code automation, brainstorming, and report drafting
Remote surveying: Shore-based control (ROC) and satellite links
IT fundamentals: Networking, cybersecurity, and troubleshooting
Soft Skills
Communication: Active listening and diplomacy
Organization: Time management and data housekeeping
Mental health: Managing stress and isolation
Networking: Mentorship and continuous learning
